admission_type: open access with mandatory admission test (free access, but requires passing a verification of personal readiness)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access (non-restricted) master's programme that requires meeting curricular credit requirements and passing a verification of the graduate's personal readiness unless exempt. Admission requires a bachelor's degree (or equivalent) of at least 180 CFU/ECTS, minimum credits in specified scientific sectors of engineering classes L-7, L-8, L-9, and English at B2 level.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A Bachelor's degree (Laurea) or an equivalent qualification with at least 180 CFU/ECTS.
academic_requirements.curricular_credit_requirements: At least 36 CFU acquired at any university in scientific areas related to classes L-7 (Civil and Environmental Engineering), L-8 (Information Engineering) and L-9 (Industrial Engineering) in the groups MAT*, FIS*, CHIM*, ING-INF/05, INF/01 and SECS-S/02, of which no fewer than 30 CFU in the basic teaching activities of MAT*, FIS*, CHIM*; AND at least 45 CFU acquired at any university in scientific areas related to classes L-7, L-8, L-9 with specified SSD codes (e.g. ICAR/01, ING-IND/11, ING-INF/01).
academic_requirements.minimum_grade_or_gpa: No minimum grade is required to apply, but a bachelor's degree final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum (i.e. about 99/110) or an ECTS classification of 'A' or 'B' exempts the candidate from the admission test.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Holders of a non-Italian degree must submit their credentials/documentation by 31 May annually; non-EU candidates residing abroad apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation.

language_requirements.required_level: English at CEFR B2 level or higher.
accepted_proofs: An official English certificate at B2 level or higher (recognised by the University, CLAT list).
Passing the University's B2 English test (University Language Centre / CLAT).
Holding a degree conducted entirely in English.
exemption_rules: A degree taught entirely in English satisfies the requirement.
Where no certificate is held, English may be assessed during the personal-readiness verification.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Verified at the admission/pre-evaluation stage, via certificate, the University B2 test, English-medium degree, or during the readiness examination.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of Adequacy of the Graduate's Personal Readiness (admission test). NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants who do not qualify for an automatic exemption.
entry_exam_or_test.format: Conducted as a public interview, a written test, or a video-interview by teleconference, assessing fundamentals of engineering and applied/professional aspects in hydraulics, hydrology, signal processing, mechanics, thermodynamics or chemistry.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: A.Y. 2026/27 sessions: 8 September 2026, 28 September 2026, 18 November 2026, 18 January 2027 and 26 February 2027.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: [uncertain] No explicit numerical passing score is published; the committee judges adequacy of preparation.
entry_exam_or_test.exemption: Candidates with a bachelor's degree final grade of at least 9/10 of the maximum, or an ECTS 'A' (or 'A'/'B') classification, are automatically exempt.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Holders of a non-Italian degree must submit credentials by 31 May (annual deadline stated on the admission-criteria page).
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: [uncertain] Programme-specific first-year enrolment dates for A.Y. 2026/27 follow the central UniGe enrolment calendar; a programme-specific date was not separately published.
application_deadline_eu.notes: This is a free-access programme; EU/resident applicants are not ranked, but must satisfy the curricular requirements and pass the admission test unless exempt.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
Engineering for Natural Risk Management is listed among the English-taught master's degrees in the UniGeApply non-EU pre-evaluation selection.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and then apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published. This programme is not among the three with a known fall-session early closure (Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Maritime Science and Technology, Computer Engineering bachelor).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window closed 20 March 2026.

required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply for non-EU pre-evaluation.
Evidence of the first-cycle qualification (180 CFU minimum).

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records (list of exams with credits and grades)
required_documents.notes: Used to verify the curricular credit requirements (36 + 45 CFU across L-7/L-8/L-9 sectors).

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2) or proof of English-medium instruction
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate, University B2 test, or a degree taught entirely in English.
Required for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Completed after UniGeApply eligibility, before applying for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: The classes are fully taught in English.
Warning! This degree course is free access, but requires passing a test.

at least 36 credits ... in the scientific areas related to the classes L-7 Civil and Environmental Engineering, L-8 Information Engineering, L-9 Industrial Engineering in the group's MAT *, FIS *, CHIM *, ING-INF / 05, INF / 01 and SECS-S / 02 of which no less than 30 CFU in the basic didactic activities of the group MAT *, FIS *, CHIM*

Candidates must demonstrate B2 level English proficiency or higher through official certificates; passing the University's B2 test; holding a degree conducted entirely in English; or assessment during the personal readiness examination.
Test dates: 08 September 2026, 28 September 2026, 18 November 2026, 18 January 2027, 26 February 2027.

uncertainty_notes: No explicit numerical passing score for the personal-readiness test is published; the committee assesses adequacy of preparation.
Programme-specific first-year EU enrolment dates were not separately published; the institution-wide UniGeApply spring window and the central enrolment calendar apply.
No programme-specific non-EU early closure is published; the programme follows the institution-wide fall close of 20 March 2026.
